assumptions: Please act as a professional verilog designer.

Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.
Module name:
RAM
Input ports:
Output ports:
Parameter:
WIDTH = 6;
DEPTH = 8;
Implementation:
RAM Array:
The module includes a register array, RAM. The array is defined as reg [DEPTH - 1 : 0] RAM [2**WIDTH-1:0], allowing for 2^6 memory locations, each with a width of 6 bits.
Write Operation:
The first always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).
On reset, indicated by !rst_n, all memory locations in the RAM array are cleared to 0.
If the write enable signal (write_en) is active, the data (write_data) is written to the RAM array at the specified address (write_addr).
Read Operation:
The second always block triggers on the positive edge of the clock signal (posedge clk) or the negative edge of the reset signal (negedge rst_n).
On reset, indicated by !rst_n, the read_data register is cleared to 0.
If the read enable signal (read_en) is active, the data at the specified address (read_addr) in the RAM array is assigned to the read_data register.
If the read enable signal is not active, the read_data register is cleared to 0.

checks.description: After reset release, observable outputs should reach a stable externally visible state before functional checking resumes.
notes: Reset functional oracle does not invent specific reset values.
checks.pass_condition: Observable outputs settle to a stable, externally usable state without relying on undocumented exact-cycle convergence.
checks.trigger_condition: When reset is released after a valid assertion interval.

notes: Sequential functional oracle remains event-based unless a future contract explicitly provides fixed latency.
checks.pass_condition: At least one observable output or progress indicator eventually updates in a manner consistent with state progress; no exact-cycle update is required.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked plan applies one or more legal sequential operations.

checks.pass_condition: After a write to address A and a simultaneous read from a different address B, read_data updates to the value previously stored at B within one clock cycle, with no X or Z values.

checks.description: Do not infer business-level completion or acceptance solely from reset activity.
notes: Clock/reset remain anchors and are not emitted as business outputs.
checks.pass_condition: No safety property is violated by treating reset as control-only; later functional claims require separate post-reset evidence.
checks.trigger_condition: While reset is asserted or being released.

notes: Default property oracle protects against over-specific later render behavior.

checks.trigger_condition: Whenever the linked case evaluates externally visible progress.

checks.description: When write_en and read_en are asserted to different addresses in the same cycle, read_data must reflect the pre‑write content of the read address after a conservative observation window.
checks.pass_condition: read_data equals the value stored at read_addr before the write occurs, with no X or Z values, observed within one clock cycle after stimulus.
checks.trigger_condition: When both write_en and read_en are high and write_addr != read_addr.

checks.description: Guardrail: repeated operations must not collapse into a single ambiguous completion claim.

checks.trigger_condition: When the linked case applies repeated or back-to-back operations.

checks.description: Treat reset assertion/release as a control event and require reset-safe interface behavior.
notes: Reset is used as an anchor only; it is not treated as a business output.
checks.pass_condition: No acceptance, completion, or externally visible functional progress is claimed solely during reset; post-release observation waits for stable, externally visible behavior.
checks.trigger_condition: When reset 'rst_n' is asserted and later released according to the inferred polarity.

notes: Reset oracle focuses on control-safe behavior instead of value-specific output claims.

unresolved_items: Reset polarity for 'clk' remains ambiguous; tests assume active‑low reset on rst_n and treat clk as a regular clock without reset polarity.
